blocks.text: Finding What Percent One Number Is of Another

blocks.body: To find what percent A is of B: divide A by B, then multiply by 100. Formula: Percent = (Part / Whole) x 100. Example: 18 is what % of 72? (18/72) x 100 = 25%.

blocks.text: Finding the Whole When a Part and Percent Are Known

blocks.body: To find the whole: divide the part by the decimal form of the percent. Formula: Whole = Part / Percent. Example: 12 is 30% of what number? 12 / 0.30 = 40.
Percent increase = (New - Original) / Original x 100. Percent decrease = (Original - New) / Original x 100. Example: price goes from $40 to $50. Percent increase = (10/40) x 100 = 25%.

blocks.label: Example
blocks.body: A shirt costs $80 and is discounted 20%. Discount = 20% x $80 = $16. Sale price = $80 - $16 = $64.
Total with tax = Original price x (1 + tax rate). Example: $50 item with 8% tax = $50 x 1.08 = $54. Tip: multiply the bill by the tip percent and add it on.
A shortcut for discounts: multiply by (1 - discount rate). A 25% off item: multiply price by 0.75. For markups: multiply by (1 + markup rate).
